lean.title: Lean FIRE
lean.reason: あなたのミニマルライフスタイルと低支出がLean FIREを実現可能に
lean.description: 倹約生活でFIをより早く達成。より少ない貯蓄で可能ですが、ライフスタイルの規律が必要です。
benefits: 従来のFIREより数年早く引退
目標達成に必要な総貯蓄が少ない
意図的な支出習慣を育成
ミニマルライフスタイルでの自由

fat.title: Fat FIRE
fat.reason: 妥協のない快適ライフスタイルへの希望がFat FIREに合致
fat.description: 豪華または上層中産階級のライフスタイルを維持してFI達成。より多くの貯蓄が必要ですが、犠牲はありません。
benefits: ライフスタイル変更なしで引退
市場下落に対する追加バッファ
旅行、食事、体験を心配なく
家族を助け、遺産を残す

barista.title: Barista FIRE
barista.reason: パートタイム勤務への興味がBarista FIREを理想的なステップに
barista.description: ポートフォリオ収入とパートタイム勤務を組み合わせ。健康保険と社会的つながりを維持しながらFIをより早く達成。
benefits: 数年早く企業の仕事を辞める
パートタイム収入で一部支出をカバー
雇用者からの健康保険
アクティブで社会的につながり続ける

coast.title: Coast FIRE
coast.reason: タイムラインと年齢がCoast FIREを戦略的アプローチに
coast.description: 今は積極的に貯蓄、その後は複利成長に任せる。柔軟性を求める若い貯蓄者に最適。
benefits: 初期に集中的に貯蓄、その後緩める
複利成長が重労働を担う
低報酬でも満足感のある仕事
30代-40代の圧力を軽減

reverse.title: リバースFIRE
reverse.reason: 具体的な引退年齢目標がターゲット貯蓄戦略を必要
reverse.description: 目標年齢から逆算して月々の貯蓄額を正確に計算。
benefits: 明確な月間貯蓄目標
目標指向のアプローチ
タイムラインまたは貯蓄を必要に応じて調整
期限駆動型プランナーに最適

savingsRate.title: 貯蓄率計算ツール
savingsRate.reason: 貯蓄率を理解することはすべてのFIREパスの基礎
savingsRate.description: 貯蓄率はFIREで最も重要な指標。この計算ツールはタイムラインへの影響を正確に表示。
benefits: より多く貯蓄する直接的影響を見る
最も重要なFIRE指標
あらゆる収入レベルで有効
財政自由への明確な道

standard.title: 標準FIRE
standard.reason: 古典的なFIREアプローチがバランスした目標とタイムラインに合致
standard.description: 従来の25倍支出ルール。証明された、バランスした財政独立アプローチ。
benefits: 時間試験された4%引き出しルール
大多数にとってバランスしたアプローチ
包括的な計画フレームワーク
従来年齢またはそれより早く引退

baristaFire.title: Barista FIRE Calculator
baristaFire.subtitle: Blend part-time work with portfolio income to retire from corporate life earlier.

whatIs.title: What is Barista FIRE?
whatIs.content: Barista FIRE (named after the stereotype of working part-time at Starbucks for benefits) means having enough invested to cover most expenses, while working a part-time or low-stress job to cover the gap and potentially access health insurance benefits.

baristaFire.partTimeTooltip: Expected yearly income from part-time work

healthInsurance.title: Health Insurance
healthInsurance.desc: Many part-time jobs offer benefits, bridging to Medicare

socialConnection.title: Social Connection
socialConnection.desc: Stay engaged with a community and routine

earlierFreedom.title: Earlier Freedom
earlierFreedom.desc: Leave your corporate job years earlier

baristaFire.content: Barista FIRE (named after the stereotype of working part-time at Starbucks for benefits) means having enough invested to cover most expenses, while working a part-time or low-stress job to cover the gap and potentially access health insurance benefits.

fatFire.title: Fat FIRE Calculator
fatFire.subtitle: Retire in style without compromising your lifestyle.

whatIs.title: What is Fat FIRE?
whatIs.content: Fat FIRE means achieving financial independence while maintaining a luxurious or upper-middle-class lifestyle (typically $100,000+/year in expenses). It requires a larger nest egg but allows you to retire without sacrifice.

fatFire.swrTooltip: Fat FIRE often uses 3.5% for extra safety

premiumHousing.title: Premium Housing
premiumHousing.desc: Nice home in desirable area, no compromise on space

travelFreedom.title: Travel Freedom
travelFreedom.desc: Multiple trips per year, business class options

generosity.title: Generosity
generosity.desc: Help family, charitable giving, leave legacy

securityBuffer.title: Security Buffer
securityBuffer.desc: Extra cushion for market downturns or surprises

leanFire.title: Lean FIRE Calculator
leanFire.subtitle: Achieve financial independence faster with a minimalist lifestyle.

whatIs.title: What is Lean FIRE?
whatIs.content: Lean FIRE means achieving financial independence with minimal expenses (typically ≤$40,000/year for a household). It requires living frugally but allows you to retire much earlier than traditional or Fat FIRE approaches.

expensesAboveThreshold.title: Expenses Above Lean Threshold
expensesAboveThreshold.content: Your expenses ({expenses}) exceed the typical Lean FIRE threshold of {threshold}. Consider the Standard FIRE or Fat FIRE calculators, or reduce your expected expenses.

housing.title: Housing
housing.desc: Consider geo-arbitrage, house hacking, or paid-off housing

transportation.title: Transportation
transportation.desc: Bike, walk, or use one reliable used car

food.title: Food
food.desc: Cook at home, meal prep, grow some food

entertainment.title: Entertainment
entertainment.desc: Free hobbies, libraries, nature, community events

leanFire.bannerContent: Lean FIRE means achieving financial independence with minimal expenses (typically ≤$40,000/year for a household). It requires living frugally but allows you to retire much earlier than traditional or Fat FIRE approaches.

coastFire.title: Coast FIRE Calculator
coastFire.subtitle: Find out how much you need now so compound growth does the rest.

whatIs.title: What is Coast FIRE?
whatIs.content: Coast FIRE is when you've saved enough that compound growth alone will get you to your full FIRE number by traditional retirement age — without any more contributions. You can then 'coast' with a lower-paying job that just covers current expenses.

coastFire.annualContributionTooltip: How much you're currently saving per year

howWorks.title: How Coast FIRE Works
howWorks.content: Your Coast FIRE Number ({coastNumber}) is the amount you need invested today such that compound growth alone will reach your full FIRE number ({fireNumber}) by age {retirementAge}.
howWorks.alreadyCoasting: Congratulations! You've already reached Coast FIRE. You could stop contributing to retirement and your portfolio would still grow to {fireNumber} by age {retirementAge}. Consider switching to work you love, even if it pays less!
howWorks.needMore: You need {needed} more to reach Coast FIRE. At your current savings rate, this will take approximately {years} years.

coastFire.bannerContent: Coast FIRE is when you've saved enough that compound growth alone will get you to your full FIRE number by traditional retirement age — without any more contributions. You can then "coast" with a lower-paying job that just covers current expenses.
coastFire.howWorksContent: Your Coast FIRE Number ({coastNumber}) is the amount you need invested today such that compound growth alone will reach your full FIRE number ({fireNumber}) by age {retirementAge}.
coastFire.alreadyCoasting: Congratulations! You've already reached Coast FIRE. You could stop contributing to retirement and your portfolio would still grow to {fireNumber} by age {retirementAge}. Consider switching to work you love, even if it pays less!

debtPayoff.title: Debt Payoff Calculator
debtPayoff.subtitle: Eliminate debt faster with Snowball or Avalanche strategies.

twoMethods.title: Two Proven Debt Payoff Methods
twoMethods.snowball: Snowball: Pay smallest debts first for quick wins and motivation.
twoMethods.avalanche: Avalanche: Pay highest interest rates first to save the most money.

noDebts.title: No debts added yet
noDebts.desc: Click 'Add Debt' to start tracking your debt payoff journey

debtPayoff.budgetMustBeAtLeast: Budget must be at least ${minPayments} (total minimum payments)

addDebtsToStart.title: Add Your Debts to Get Started
addDebtsToStart.desc: Enter your debt information in the left panel to see your payoff strategy.

debtPayoff.snowballDesc: Pay off debts from smallest to largest balance, regardless of interest rate. This method provides quick wins and builds momentum as you eliminate debts one by one. Best for staying motivated on your debt-free journey.
debtPayoff.avalancheDesc: Pay off debts from highest to lowest interest rate, regardless of balance. This method saves you the most money on interest over time and is mathematically optimal. Best for maximizing long-term savings.

snowballVsAvalanche.title: Snowball vs Avalanche Comparison
snowballVsAvalanche.winner: Winner: {method} saves {savings} in interest and finishes {monthsDiff} months {direction}.

debtPayoff.addDebtsTitle: Add Your Debts to Get Started
debtPayoff.addDebtsDesc: Enter your debt information in the left panel to see your payoff strategy.
debtPayoff.totalMonthlyBudgetTooltip: Total amount you can pay toward debts each month

healthcareGap.title: Healthcare Gap Calculator
healthcareGap.subtitle: Estimate healthcare costs between early retirement and Medicare eligibility.

warning.title: The Healthcare Gap is Real
warning.content: If you retire before age 65 in the US, you'll need to bridge the gap until Medicare. Healthcare costs are often the #1 overlooked expense in early retirement planning. This can add {cost} or more to your FIRE number!

healthcareGap.earlyRetirementAgeTooltip: When you plan to leave employer-sponsored insurance
healthcareGap.healthcareCostEstimatesNote: Defaults based on ACA Silver plan averages. Adjust based on your state and needs.

acaSubsidyPotential.title: ACA Subsidy Potential
acaSubsidyPotential.desc: Your retirement income affects ACA premium subsidies. Lower income = higher subsidies.

healthcareGap.acaSubsidyNote: * Subsidies are estimates based on simplified calculations. Actual amounts depend on your state, household size, plan type, and exact income. Check healthcare.gov for accurate quotes.

baristaFire.title: Barista FIRE
baristaFire.desc: Part-time job with health benefits (Starbucks, Costco, etc.)

hsaBridge.title: HSA Bridge
hsaBridge.desc: Max out HSA while working, use tax-free in retirement

incomeManagement.title: Income Management
incomeManagement.desc: Keep income low to qualify for ACA subsidies

geoArbitrage.title: Geo-Arbitrage
geoArbitrage.desc: Retire abroad with affordable healthcare options

healthcareGap.acaSubsidyDesc: Your retirement income affects ACA premium subsidies. Lower income = higher subsidies.

reverseFire.title: Reverse FIRE Calculator
reverseFire.subtitle: Find out how much you need to save monthly to FIRE by your target age.

howWorks.title: How Reverse FIRE Works
howWorks.content: Instead of calculating when you'll FIRE based on your savings, this calculator works backwards: you set your target retirement age, and it tells you exactly how much you need to save each month to get there.

reverseFire.targetFireAgeTooltip: When do you want to achieve FIRE?
reverseFire.onTrackDesc: Your current savings of {savings} will grow to {willGrow} by age {targetAge}, exceeding your FIRE number of {fireNumber}.
reverseFire.toFireByAgeYouNeed: To FIRE by age {targetAge}, you need to save

reverseFire.toFireByAgeDesc: To FIRE by age {targetAge}, you need to save

savingsRate.title: Savings & Investment Rate Calculator
savingsRate.subtitle: See how your investments can grow over time with consistent contributions.

powerOfCompound.title: The Power of Compound Interest
powerOfCompound.content: Consistent investing is the key to building wealth. Even small amounts invested regularly can grow substantially over time thanks to compound interest. Start early and stay consistent!

savingsRate.compareNominalVsInflation: Compare nominal growth vs. inflation-adjusted purchasing power
savingsRate.inflationAdjustedValueNote: Note: Inflation will reduce your purchasing power by {impact} over {years} years at {rate}% annual inflation.
savingsRate.compoundInterestContent: Consistent investing is the key to building wealth. Even small amounts invested regularly can grow substantially over time thanks to compound interest. Start early and stay consistent!

withdrawalRate.title: Withdrawal Rate Calculator
withdrawalRate.subtitle: Test your portfolio's longevity and find your safe withdrawal rate.

fourPercentRule.title: The 4% Rule
fourPercentRule.content: The famous '4% rule' from the Trinity Study suggests withdrawing 4% of your portfolio in year one, then adjusting for inflation each year. This has historically provided a 95%+ success rate over 30-year retirements. However, longer retirements may require lower rates (3-3.5%).

withdrawalRate.withdrawalRateTooltip: Percentage of portfolio to withdraw yearly
withdrawalRate.retirementDurationTooltip: How many years you need the portfolio to last

rateAnalysis.title: Withdrawal Rate Analysis
rateAnalysis.desc: Compare different withdrawal rates for your portfolio of {portfolioValue}

recommendations.title: Recommendations

sustainable.title: Your withdrawal rate is sustainable!
sustainable.content: At {rate}%, your portfolio of {portfolioValue} should last {longevity} years, meeting your {retirementYears}-year goal.

considerLowering.title: Consider lowering your withdrawal rate.
considerLowering.content: At {rate}%, your portfolio would only last {longevity} years. Try reducing to 3.5% or 3% for a longer-lasting portfolio.

recommendations.earlyRetirees: For early retirees (40+ year retirements): Consider a 3-3.5% withdrawal rate for extra safety margin.
recommendations.flexibleSpending: Flexible spending: Being willing to reduce spending during market downturns significantly improves success rates.

withdrawalRate.fourPercentRuleContent: The famous "4% rule" from the Trinity Study suggests withdrawing 4% of your portfolio in year one, then adjusting for inflation each year. This has historically provided a 95%+ success rate over 30-year retirements. However, longer retirements may require lower rates (3-3.5%).

about.title: About FIRE Calculator
about.subtitle: A free, privacy-first tool for planning your path to financial independence

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: To provide accessible, privacy-respecting financial planning tools that help everyone pursue financial independence — without compromising their personal data.

about.whatIsFireContent: FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early) is a lifestyle movement focused on achieving financial freedom through aggressive saving and smart investing. The goal is to accumulate enough wealth that your investments can cover your living expenses indefinitely, giving you the freedom to retire much earlier than traditional retirement age.
about.corePrincipleContent: The "4% Rule" suggests that if you withdraw 4% of your portfolio annually, your savings should last 30+ years. This means you need approximately 25x your annual expenses to achieve FIRE.
about.goalContent: Financial independence means having enough passive income (from investments) to cover your living expenses, giving you freedom to choose how you spend your time — whether that's early retirement, part-time work, or pursuing passion projects.

standard.title: Standard FIRE
standard.desc: The classic 25x annual expenses approach. Full financial independence with no need for additional income.

coast.title: Coast FIRE
coast.desc: Save enough now so compound growth carries you to traditional retirement. Stop saving early and "coast" to FI.

lean.title: Lean FIRE
lean.desc: FI with a frugal lifestyle. Lower expenses mean a smaller target number, achieved faster.

fat.title: Fat FIRE
fat.desc: FI with a comfortable or luxurious lifestyle. Higher expenses require a larger portfolio.

barista.title: Barista FIRE
barista.desc: Partial FI combined with part-time work. Leave full-time corporate work earlier while maintaining some income.

about.toolsIntro: We offer a comprehensive suite of calculators to help you plan every aspect of your FIRE journey:

standardFire.title: Standard FIRE Calculator
standardFire.desc: Calculate your FIRE number and timeline to full financial independence.

coastFire.title: Coast FIRE Calculator
coastFire.desc: Find your Coast FIRE number — when you can stop saving and let compound growth finish the job.

leanFatFire.title: Lean & Fat FIRE
leanFatFire.desc: Calculate FI targets for minimalist or comfortable lifestyles.

baristaFire.title: Barista FIRE Calculator
baristaFire.desc: Blend part-time work with investment income for earlier semi-retirement.

withdrawalRate.title: Withdrawal Rate Calculator
withdrawalRate.desc: Test portfolio longevity and find your safe withdrawal rate.

savingsRate.title: Savings Rate Calculator
savingsRate.desc: Calculate your savings rate and see how it impacts your FIRE timeline.

free.title: 100% Free
free.desc: No subscriptions, no premium tiers, no hidden fees. All calculators are completely free to use.

privacy.title: Privacy First
privacy.desc: Your financial data stays in your browser. No accounts, no databases, no server processing.

offline.title: Works Offline
offline.desc: After initial load, the app works without internet. Install as a PWA and calculate anywhere.

noTracking.title: No Tracking
noTracking.desc: No analytics, no cookies, no third-party trackers. Your usage is completely private.

shareable.title: Shareable URLs
shareable.desc: Save calculations in URLs. Bookmark, share with a partner, or revisit later — your data is in the link.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.desc: All code is publicly available. Verify our privacy claims yourself — transparency is key.

openSource.content: This calculator is open source, meaning anyone can inspect, verify, and contribute to the code. We believe transparency builds trust — especially for financial tools.
openSource.note: Feel free to explore the code, report issues, suggest features, or contribute improvements. We welcome community involvement and feedback.

about.learnMoreIntro: Explore our curated resources to deepen your understanding of financial independence:

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator is for educational and informational purposes only. It is not financial, investment, tax, or legal advice. Results are hypothetical projections based on user inputs and assumed rates. Actual results will vary. Please consult a qualified financial advisor before making any financial decisions.
disclaimer.link: Read our full Terms of Service

whoWeAre.title: Who We Are
whoWeAre.content: FIRE Calculator is an open-source project built by and for the FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early) community. We're a small team of developers and financial enthusiasts who believe everyone deserves access to quality financial planning tools.
whoWeAre.teamDesc: We're volunteers and contributors from around the world, united by our passion for financial education and privacy-first technology.

whyFree.title: Why This is Free
whyFree.content: We believe financial planning tools should be accessible to everyone. Here's how we keep the lights on while staying free:

ads.title: Ethical Advertising
ads.desc: We display Google AdSense ads to cover server costs. No tracking, no data selling.

affiliates.title: Affiliate Links
affiliates.desc: Book recommendations include Amazon affiliate links. You pay nothing extra.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.desc: Community contributions help improve and maintain the project.

offline.title: Offline-First
offline.desc: Works without internet after initial load. Install as a PWA on any device.

privacy.title: Privacy-First
privacy.desc: All calculations happen in your browser. We never see or store your data.

shareable.title: Shareable URLs
shareable.desc: Save your calculations in the URL. Bookmark, share, or revisit anytime.

translations.title: Multi-Language
translations.desc: Available in 10+ languages with community-contributed translations.

accurate.title: Accurate Math
accurate.desc: Real-world formulas used by financial planners. No approximations.

mobile.title: Mobile-Friendly
mobile.desc: Responsive design works great on phones, tablets, and desktops.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.content: This project is fully open source. You can:

actions.fork: Fork it for your own projects

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.content: Have questions, suggestions, or just want to say hi? We'd love to hear from you.

summary.title: TL;DR: Your Privacy is Our Priority

points.browserSide: All calculations happen 100% in your browser
points.noCollection: We do not collect, store, or transmit any personal or financial information
points.noAccounts: No user accounts, no databases, no server-side processing
points.noTracking: No tracking, no analytics, no cookies for user data

infoCollect.title: 1. Information We Collect
infoCollect.intro: We do not collect any personal or financial information. This FIRE Calculator is designed with privacy as a core principle. Here's what that means:

items.names: Names, email addresses, or contact information

howCalculations.title: 2. How Your Calculations Work
howCalculations.intro: All calculations are performed entirely within your web browser (client-side). When you enter financial information:

browserSide.title: Browser-Side Processing
browserSide.desc: All mathematical calculations happen in JavaScript running in your browser. No data is sent to our servers.

urlStorage.title: URL-Based Storage (Optional)
urlStorage.desc: Your calculation inputs are encoded in the URL when you share or bookmark. This is the only way your data persists — and it's entirely under your control.

noServer.title: No Server Transmission
noServer.desc: Your financial data is never transmitted to our servers for processing, storage, or any other purpose.

localStorage.title: 3. Local Storage Usage
localStorage.intro: We use browser local storage to save non-sensitive preferences only. This data never leaves your device:
localStorage.clearNote: You can clear this data at any time through your browser settings. Clearing local storage will not affect your calculations — it only resets your preferences to defaults.

googleAdsense.title: Google AdSense
googleAdsense.intro: We use Google AdSense to display advertisements. Google AdSense may collect certain information to serve relevant ads, including:
googleAdsense.important: Important: Google's ad tracking is independent of our calculator. Your financial calculations and inputs are never shared with Google or any advertiser.

amazonAffiliate.title: Amazon Affiliate Links
amazonAffiliate.content: Some book recommendations include Amazon affiliate links. When you click these links, Amazon may collect data according to their privacy policy. This does not affect your calculator usage or data.

noAnalytics.title: No Google Analytics
noAnalytics.desc: We don't use Google Analytics or similar tracking tools.

noUserTracking.title: No User Tracking
noUserTracking.desc: We don't track individual users or sessions.

noCrossSite.title: No Cross-Site Tracking
noCrossSite.desc: We don't follow you across other websites.

dataSecurity.title: 6. Data Security
dataSecurity.content: Since all calculations happen locally in your browser and we don't collect any data, there's no database to breach. Your financial information is as secure as your own device. We recommend:

recommendations.device: Keeping your device protected with a password or biometric lock
recommendations.urls: Being cautious when sharing URLs that contain your financial calculations

yourRights.title: 7. Your Rights
yourRights.intro: Because we don't collect or store your personal data, many data protection rights are automatically satisfied:

rights.access: Right to Access: All your data is in your browser — you have complete access.
rights.deletion: Right to Deletion: Clear your browser data to delete everything.
rights.portability: Right to Portability: Your calculation URLs contain all your data in portable form.
rights.object: Right to Object: No processing to object to — your data stays with you.

changes.title: 8. Changes to This Policy
changes.content: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. We will notify users of any material changes by posting the new policy on this page with an updated "Last updated" date.

contact.title: 9. Contact Us
contact.content: If you have questions about this privacy policy, you can:
contact.aboutPageSuffix: for more information about the project

agreement.title: 1. Agreement to Terms
agreement.content: By accessing and using FIRE Calculator (available at firecalc.nextapi.pro and myfirenumber.com), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our service.

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator is provided for educational and informational purposes only.

items.notAdvice: It is NOT financial, investment, tax, or legal advice.
items.hypothetical: Results are hypothetical projections based on user inputs and assumed rates.
items.vary: Actual investment returns, inflation rates, and outcomes will vary.
items.past: Past performance does not guarantee future results.
items.consult: You should consult with qualified professionals before making any financial decisions.

disclaimer.notResponsible: We are not responsible for any financial decisions you make based on calculations from this tool.

serviceDescription.title: 2. Description of Service
serviceDescription.content: FIRE Calculator provides free, browser-based financial planning calculators for the FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early) community. Our services include:

items.calculators: Various FIRE calculators (Standard, Coast, Lean, Fat, Barista)
items.withdrawal: Withdrawal rate and savings rate calculators
items.reverse: Reverse FIRE and debt payoff calculators

serviceDescription.allClientSide: All calculations are performed client-side (in your browser). We do not store your financial data on our servers.

notProfessionalAdvice.title: 3. Not Professional Advice
notProfessionalAdvice.content: The information provided by FIRE Calculator does not constitute:

financial.title: Financial Advice
financial.desc: We do not recommend specific investments, savings strategies, or financial products.

investment.title: Investment Advice
investment.desc: We do not advise on buying, selling, or holding any securities or assets.

tax.title: Tax Advice
tax.desc: We do not provide guidance on tax strategies or obligations.

legal.title: Legal Advice
legal.desc: We do not provide legal guidance on any matters.

notProfessionalAdvice.consult: Always consult with qualified professionals (financial advisors, tax professionals, attorneys) before making financial decisions.

userResponsibilities.title: 4. User Responsibilities
userResponsibilities.intro: By using our service, you agree to:

items.personal: Use the calculator for personal, non-commercial purposes
items.noReverse: Not attempt to reverse engineer, copy, or redistribute the software
items.noScrape: Not use automated tools to scrape or abuse the service
items.verify: Verify all calculations with qualified professionals before making financial decisions
items.accept: Accept responsibility for your own financial decisions

ourRights.title: Our Rights
ourRights.content: The FIRE Calculator software, including its design, code, logos, and content (excluding third-party content and user-generated data), is protected by intellectual property laws. While the core calculator code is open source, the following restrictions apply:

items.view: You may view and fork the open-source code on GitHub
items.branding: You may not use our branding, logos, or domain names without permission
items.claim: You may not claim the calculator as your own work
items.derivative: Derivative works must comply with the applicable open-source license

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Content
thirdParty.content: Some content on our site, such as book recommendations and affiliate links, may link to third-party websites. We are not responsible for the content, privacy practices, or terms of third-party sites.

limitationOfLiability.title: 6. Limitation of Liability
limitationOfLiability.intro: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW:

noWarranty.title: No Warranty
noWarranty.desc: The calculator is provided "AS IS" without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to accuracy, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

noGuarantee.title: No Guarantee of Results
noGuarantee.desc: We do not guarantee that calculations will be accurate or that any financial outcomes will be achieved.

limitation.title: Limitation of Damages
limitation.desc: We shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of the calculator.

disclaimerOutcomes.title: 7. Disclaimer of Financial Outcomes
disclaimerOutcomes.intro: The projections and calculations provided by FIRE Calculator are based on:

items.inputs: User-provided inputs which may be inaccurate
items.assumed: Assumed rates (investment returns, inflation) that may not reflect reality
items.historical: Historical data that may not predict future performance
items.simplified: Simplified models that do not account for all real-world factors

disclaimerOutcomes.actualDepends: Actual financial outcomes depend on numerous factors beyond our control, including but not limited to: market conditions, tax laws, healthcare costs, inflation, unexpected expenses, and individual circumstances. Never rely solely on this calculator for financial planning.

googleAdsense.title: Google AdSense
googleAdsense.content: We use Google AdSense to display advertisements. Google may use cookies and similar technologies to serve ads. Your use of our service is also subject to Google's terms and privacy policy.

affiliate.title: Affiliate Links
affiliate.content: Some links on our site (such as book recommendations) are affiliate links. If you make a purchase through these links, we may receive a small commission at no additional cost to you. This helps support the free calculator. We are not responsible for the products, services, or terms of third-party vendors.

changes.title: 9. Changes to Terms
changes.content: We reserve the right to modify these Terms of Service at any time. Changes will be effective immediately upon posting on this page. Your continued use of the service after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms. We encourage you to review these terms periodically.
changes.material: Material changes will be indicated by updating the "Last updated" date at the top of this page.

termination.title: 10. Termination
termination.content: We reserve the right to terminate or suspend access to our service at any time, without notice, for any reason, including but not limited to violation of these Terms. Since this is a free, client-side application with no user accounts, termination means you may no longer be able to access our website.

governingLaw.title: 11. Governing Law
governingLaw.content: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with applicable laws, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any disputes arising from these terms or use of the service shall be resolved in the appropriate courts of jurisdiction.

contact.title: 12. Contact Us
contact.content: If you have questions about these Terms of Service, please:

acknowledgment.title: Acknowledgment
acknowledgment.content: By using FIRE Calculator, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms of Service, including the important disclaimers about financial advice.

home.title: FIRE計算機
home.subtitle: 経済的独立と早期退職への道のりを計画しましょう。無料、プライベート、完全にオフラインで動作します。

quiz.title: どの計算機を使えばいいかわからない？
quiz.desc: あなたの状況に最適なFIREパスを見つけるためのクイッククイズに参加してください。いくつかの質問に答えると、情報が事前入力された最適な計算機をおすすめします。
quiz.cta: あなたのFIREパスを見つける
quiz.note: 2〜3分かかります · パーソナライズされた推奨

books.title: おすすめのFIRE書籍
books.subtitle: 経済的独立への旅を加速する必読の書。
books.viewAll: すべての本と詳細を見る
books.affiliateNote: アフィリエイトリンク — 購入は追加費用なしでこの無料計算機をサポートします。

privacy.title: あなたのプライバシーが最優先
privacy.subtitle: この計算機はプライバシーファーストの原則で構築されました。

noStorage.title: 財務データの非保存
noStorage.desc: あなたの財務データはURLにのみ存在し、保存されません。UI設定（テーマ、レイアウト）のみがローカルに保存されます。

noAnalytics.title: アナリティクスなし
noAnalytics.desc: トラッキングスクリプトゼロ。Google Analyticsなし、サードパーティコードなし。

urlBased.title: URLベースの共有
urlBased.desc: 計算をURLに保存。ブックマークまたは共有 — あなたの選択。

worksOffline.title: オフラインで動作
worksOffline.desc: 初回読み込み後、インターネットなしで動作します。アプリとしてデバイスにインストール可能。

openSource.title: オープンソース
openSource.desc: すべてを自分で確認できます。全コードがGitHubで公開されています。

clientSide.title: クライアント側のみ
clientSide.desc: すべての計算がブラウザで実行されます。サーバー処理なし。

whatIsFire.title: FIREとは？
whatIsFire.para1: FIREはFinancial Independence, Retire Early（経済的独立、早期退職）の略です。従来の退職年齢よりはるかに早く退職するために、極端な貯蓄と投資に焦点を当てた財務運動です。核心原則はシンプルです：積極的に貯蓄し、賢明に投資し、投資が生活費を無期限にカバーできるようになると、経済的独立を達成します。
whatIsFire.para2: 最も一般的なFIRE計算は4%ルール（または25倍ルール）を使用します：ポートフォリオの4%で生活できる場合、年間支出の25倍を貯蓄する必要があります。例えば、年間$40,000を使う場合、経済的に独立するには$1,000,000が必要です。

tallyAi.title: Tally AIで進捗を追跡
tallyAi.desc: 支出の追跡、予算の管理、AI駆動の洞察でFIRE目標を達成するのをサポートするスマートな財務パートナー。これらの計算機への完璧な補完。

disclaimer.title: 免責事項
disclaimer.content: この計算機は教育および情報提供のみを目的としています。財務、投資、税務、法的アドバイスではありません。結果は仮想的な予測であり、実際の結果は異なります。財務上の決定を下す前に、適格な財務アドバイザーに相談してください。

home.builtWith: プライバシーを考慮して構築。データはブラウザから出ることはありません。

noAnalytics.desc: トラッキングスクリプトゼロ。Google Analyticsなし、サードパーティコードなし。

urlActions.resetTitle: Clear all values (URL and saved data)

disclaimer.title: ⚠️ Disclaimer
disclaimer.para1: This calculator is provided for educational and informational purposes only. It is not intended to be, and should not be construed as, financial, investment, tax, or legal advice.
disclaimer.para2: The results shown are hypothetical projections based on the inputs you provide and assumptions about future returns, inflation, and other factors. Actual results will vary — past performance does not guarantee future results.
disclaimer.para3: Before making any financial decisions, please consult with a qualified financial advisor, tax professional, or other appropriate professional who can consider your individual circumstances.

standardFire.title: Standard FIRE Calculator
standardFire.subtitle: Calculate your path to financial independence using the 25x expenses rule.
standardFire.targetRetirementAgeTooltip: The age you want to retire
standardFire.annualNetIncomeTooltip: Your net (take-home) annual income after taxes

apps.title: Recommended FIRE Apps
apps.subtitle: Essential apps to accelerate your financial independence journey.
apps.smartToolsDesc: These apps complement your FIRE calculators with practical tools for budgeting, tracking, and managing your finances. More recommendations coming soon!
apps.disclaimerContent: We are not affiliated with or endorsed by any of the apps listed above. These recommendations are provided for informational purposes only. We only recommend apps we genuinely believe will help you on your FIRE journey. Please do your own research before using any third-party service.

books.title: Recommended FIRE Books
books.subtitle: Essential reading for your financial independence journey.
books.knowledgeDesc: These books have helped millions achieve financial independence. Whether you're just starting out or optimizing your FIRE strategy, there's something here for everyone.
books.affiliateContent: The links above are Amazon affiliate links. If you purchase through these links, we may earn a small commission at no additional cost to you. This helps support the development of this free calculator. We only recommend books we genuinely believe will help you on your FIRE journey.
